title: Greenfield vs brownfield
intro: Quick check after the definitions above.
feedbackIncorrect: Review the definitions — try again on the next page if needed.

questions.prompt: Which best describes a brownfield project?
choices: Building on top of an existing production system and its constraints
Starting from a blank repository with no legacy code
A project that only uses brown as the primary brand color

questions.prompt: Which is most characteristic of greenfield work?
choices: You must preserve every existing API for backward compatibility
You choose stack and architecture without inheriting an old codebase
You are forbidden from using new frameworks

questions.prompt: Which is typically a stronger constraint in brownfield projects?
choices: Legacy code, existing APIs, and uptime expectations
Choosing a brand color from scratch
Having no stakeholders or users yet

questions.prompt: In greenfield development, you often have more freedom to:
choices: Define stack and project structure from a clean slate
